_meta.purpose: Single editable source of truth for the day orchestrator. Cadence, priority, and freshness are deliberately KNOBS — tuned by hand during the ~1-week parallel dry-run (Megan 2026-06-23).
_meta.scope: ALL non-upload reports run on the scheduler (Megan 2026-06-23). Frontier + Financial moved to auto email-ingest 2026-07-01. First/Last Sale RETIRED 2026-07-06.
_meta.weekday_convention: Python weekday(): Mon=0, Tue=1, Wed=2, Thu=3, Fri=4, Sat=5, Sun=6 (matches dashboard.py).
_meta.times: All times are CST (the mini is Central). HH:MM 24h.
_meta.priority_tiers: P1 critical (guaranteed done daily, runs first) > P2 standard > P3 best-effort (runs last).
_meta.freshness_target: A DEADLINE distinct from cadence. e.g. 'monday_am' = must be current by Monday morning even if deprioritized midweek; the orchestrator escalates effective priority as the deadline nears.
_meta.source_type: tableau = readiness gate + circle-back applies | appstream = immediately ready (no probe) | api = ready | upload = MANUAL, never auto-run.
_meta.run_order_flow: Run order optimizes TOTAL runtime (registry.flow_rank): wave 1 = every NON-tableau report (appstream / api / email / upload) so their early-ready data fills first; wave 2 = all source_type 'tableau' reports LAST, giving Tableau the most time to finish publishing before we read it; daily_rep_breakdown is pinned dead last (heaviest, flakiest). New reports auto-map by source_type — set it correctly and the report lands in the right wave automatically; no per-report order field. Source-type-based, never weekday-based, so a weekend-scheduled AppStream report still runs in wave 1 and is never blocked.
_meta.probe_not_configured: A tableau source with probe.type 'not_configured' resolves READY in --dry-run (so the full loop exercises) and NOT-READY in live (forces wiring the real view URL before cutover).
_meta.cutover_retire_jobs: These now run from the orchestrator — RETIRE their standalone LaunchAgents at cutover so reports don't double-run/double-post: com.alphalete.appstream-morning (daily_focus + Mon recruiter retention), com.alphalete.weather-6am, com.alphalete.brand-audit-noon, com.alphalete.recruiting-report (weekly ATT). Until cutover (dry-run week) they coexist safely — the orchestrator writes nothing.

READ-ONLY check of a past week: `lucy rerun drb_tableau_pull --week-ending 2026-07-12 --fill --dry-run` — step7 passes --dry-run through to step6 (step7:477) and step6 only writes `if cells and not dry_run` (step6:296), so it downloads + reports 'would write' per owner and touches NO cells. ⚠ NEVER pass --fill WITHOUT --dry-run for a PAST week: step6 maps the crosstab onto the CURRENT week's day columns, so that would write last week's production into this week's block on all 28 tabs. Registered 2026-07-07.

rebuild_lastweek._note: MANUAL-ONLY recovery — registered so it can run on the MINI via lucy (the ownerville session lives there; a laptop scrape knocks out the session every other report shares). Replays the known-good Monday sequence for a target week: label top as last week -> scrape last week (ownerville) -> [Tableau, full run only] -> FREEZE (HARD-GATED: aborts before the wipe if the freeze writes 0 tabs) -> label top as this week -> wipe -> scrape this week. Use when a tab's frozen LAST WEEK block is STRUCTURALLY broken (nested/duplicate 'LAST WEEK' labels, reps stranded far below) — drb_backfill_lastweek only refills activity by rep name and CANNOT fix structure. Preview: `lucy rerun rebuild_lastweek --monday 2026-07-06 --plan`. One tab: `lucy rerun rebuild_lastweek --monday 2026-07-06 --only '"Kiarri McBroom"'` (note the literal quotes — lucy joins argv, shlex re-splits). WARNING: --only SKIPS Tableau (org-wide, unscopable), so the rebuilt frozen block has NO SALES — a frozen block is a static snapshot and can't be sales-patched after. Only use --only on a tab you've confirmed had zero sales that week. Registered 2026-07-07.
rebuild_lastweek.display_name: Daily Rep Breakdown — rebuild frozen LAST WEEK block

org_sales_board._note: Fills the SANDBOX 'Copy of' tab daily, FILL-ONLY — the Tuesday auto-rollover was removed, so the weekly rollover is MANUAL (a person Monday night). Auto-compares vs the VA tab. Intentionally targets the sandbox; the real tab stays guarded (see _warn). TIMING (Megan 2026-07-11): runs in its ORDER (8), gated on DATA readiness — the not_before '08:00' clock was removed. The board's last-landing source is BOX (extract refreshes ~7-8am); data_sources now includes 'tableau:box_daily' with a box_daily readiness probe, so the orchestrator holds the board until Box has the latest completed day, then it runs (no more fixed 8am wait when Box is in early). The email (org_sales_board_email) depends_on this, so it follows right after. The 2:30pm com.alphalete.board-catchup remains the backstop. Bootstraps com.alphalete.orchestrator-schedule-guard, a launchd job that fires DAILY at 02:45 (before the earliest daily job, appstream-morning at 03:00) and runs schedule_guard.py, which re-bootstraps EVERY loaded, timed com.alphalete.* job from its current plist. WHY: `git pull` updates a plist FILE but never reloads launchd, so launchd kept firing stale boot-time schedules — the 4am orchestrator fired 6am for 15 days (2026-07-07/-08/-09, 4 recurrences) and brand-audit-noon once fired 2pm; every timed job carries the same risk. The guard reloads them all every night so launchd can NEVER hold a stale one and nobody has to remember to reinstall. Skips KeepAlive/always-on jobs and high-frequency pollers (rc-autoread, resume-pushing). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'. Safe/idempotent; no committed plist has RunAtLoad so neither installing the guard nor its nightly reloads fire any job immediately. Run once per machine to install; re-run any time to refresh the guard itself.

box_order_log._off_scheduler_note: Carlos's BOX (B2B) Order Log -> the 'Lucy Box Order Log' tab on the Vantura Master Sales Board. on_scheduler:false = NOT in the 4am batch; the real schedule is a STANDALONE LaunchAgent (com.alphalete.box-order-log) TWICE daily incl. weekends at 7:00am and 8:30am CST (Carlos asked for those two exactly, 2026-07-18). EXACTLY ONE Slack post per day: box_order_log.sh writes a dated marker after a successful post, so the 8:30 pass sees it and refreshes the sheet ONLY. If 7:00 dies before posting, no marker is written and 8:30 posts instead — never two. Present here so `lucy rerun box_order_log` works. Pulls the BoxOrderLog view, collapses the status-transition rows into one row per sale (sale key = Contract ID + Account Id; Contract ID alone is NOT unique), and MERGES into a rolling 6-week log: new sales appended, status changes updated in place, oldest week aged out. Merge is idempotent — a second pass on unchanged data reports '0 new, 0 status changes'. The Tableau view is a rolling ~44-day window with only ~3 days of slack over 6 weeks, which is WHY it merges instead of overwriting: rows age out of the source before they age out of the report. TWO deliverables per run, different scopes on purpose: --sheet merges the rolling 6-week log into the board; --xlsx writes the day's FULL pull to output/ as one tab per rep (Fiber-style, mirrors order_log._append_rep_breakdown_tabs). DRY by default. Does NOT post to Slack. Belongs on LUCY 2 (Carlos's machine). Registered 2026-07-18.
